rules: Flag any hardcoded secrets, connection strings, or API keys.
Flag any SQL built by string concatenation — always require parameterized queries.
Flag endpoints or methods that expose sensitive data without an authorization check.
Flag any service that directly opens a database connection to another service's database — each service must only access its own data store.
config.context: eShop reference application — a .NET microservices e-commerce system with Basket, Catalog, Ordering, Identity, and Payment services.

public record ShipOrderCommand(int OrderNumber) : IRequest<bool>;

expectedFindings.description: Hardcoded database credentials in source code
Hardcoded database credentials in source code
Wrong argument in SendShippingNotification call — BuyerName as email and/or Description as tracking number
IDOR: no verification that caller owns the order before updating tracking
Logic bug: checks if ANY product available instead of ALL
Enum ordinal shift: OrderTrackingUpdated inserted at 2, shifts OrderShipped and OrderPaid values
DDD violation: SetTrackingNumber has no status guard unlike SetShippedStatus
Inconsistent pattern: uses manual HttpClient instead of IWebhooksRetriever/IWebhooksSender like other handlers
